DISCOVERY 612, PRESENT: KIMBERLY A. FANADY, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY. KRISTINA H. CHUNG, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY. MOTION ARGUED. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATION. TENTATIVE RULING: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS IN RESPONSE TO PLAINTIFF'S THIRD DOCUMENT REQUEST IS DENIED AS CIVIL CODE SECTION 47 ESTABLISHES AN ABSOLUTE PRIVILEGE, SHIELDING A REPORT TO LAW ENFORCEMENT CONCERNING SUSPECTED CRIMINAL ACTIVITY. [SEE HAGBERG V. CALIFORNIA FEDERAL BANK FSB, 32 CAL.4TH 350, 375] THAT PRIVILEGE SHEILDS THE REQUESTED DOCUMENTS, ASSUMING THAT PLAINTIFF'S BELIEF IS CORRECT, FROM DISCOVERY. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO THE KIPPERMAN DECLARATION ARE SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART: AS TO PARAGRAPH 3, THE OBJECTION IS SUSTAINED; AS TO PARAGRAPH 7, THE OBJECTION IS OVERRULED; AS TO PARAGRAPH 9, THE HEARSAY OBJECTION IS SUSTAINED; AS TO PARAGRAPH 10, THE HEARSAY OBJECTION IS SUSTAINED; AS TO PARAGRAPH 12, THE PHRASE "AS COMMUNICATED IN AND/OR EVIDENCED BY SAID DOCUMENTS, IS STRICKEN AS IT IS HEARSAY; AS TO PARAGRAPH 13, THE HEARSAY OBJECTION IS SUSTAINED AND EXHIBIT 3 IS STRICKEN; AS TO PARAGRAPHS 14-17, THE OBJECTION IS OVERRULED. MODIFICATION: COURT ALSO FINDS THAT THE MATERIALS ARE PRIVILEGED WORK PRODUCT. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

DISCOVERY 612, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS ORDERED CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 17, 2004 AT 9 A.M. FOR FURTHER BRIEFING AND FOR FURTHER MEET AND CONFER. THE OPPOSITION HAS NOT OFFERED ANY DECLARATIONS IN SUPPORT OF ANY CLAIMS OF PRIVILEGE. SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFS AND DECLARATIONS SHALL BE MADE WHICH NARROW THE MATTERS THAT REMAIN IN DISPUTE AND WHICH SUPPORT ANY CLAIMS OF PRIVILEGE. DEFENDANT'S SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATIONS SHALL BE FILED NO LATER THAN SEPTEMBER 3, 2004. PLAINTIFF'S SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF SHALL BE FILED NO LATER THAN SEPTEMBER 10, 2004. DEFENDANT'S SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION SHALL BE FILED NO LATER THAN SEPTEMBER 15, 2004. COURTESY COPIES OF ALL SUPPLEMENTAL PAPERS SHALL BE LODGED IN ROOM 633. (612/EAHEWLETT)

DISCOVERY, 612, APPEARANCES: KIMBERLY A. FANADY, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY, KREUGER BROTHERS BUILDERS, INC. KRISTINA H. CHUNG, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY, GOLDEN EAGLE INSURANCE CORPORATION. MATTER ARGUED. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWR: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS DENIED. WHILE THE DECLARATION OF HOWARD S. VALLENS MIGHT HAVE BEEN MORE SPECIFIC, IT IS SUFFICIENT. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
